LAW AND MOTION, 301, MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD FOR PLAINTIFF LISA MALEPEAI IS OFF CALENDAR. LOCAL RULE 8.2(A)(2)(B) PROHIBITS SETTING A MOTION IN THIS DEPARTMENT ON THE SAME DAY A MOTION IS SET IN ANOTHER DEPARTMENT. JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH, REPORTER: CAROL KAREN, CSR# 8189

DISCOVERY 612, DEFENDANT'S MOTION TO COMPEL RESPONSES TO FORM INTERROGATORIES, SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES, REQUEST FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS, AND REQUEST FOR STATEMENT OF DAMAGES IS GRANTED. SANCTIONS ARE IMPOSED AGAINST PLAINTIFF IN THE AMOUNT OF $320, TO BE PAID WITHIN 30 DAYS OF NOTICE OF THIS ORDER. COMM: EVERETT A. HEWLETT. MATTER NOT REPORTED (SEE MINI MINUTES FOR DETAILS).

LAW AND MOTION 301, JUDGE BUSCH MADE A DISCLOSURE ABOUT HIS WIFE, CATHARINE BARNES, A DEPUTY CITY ATTORNEY NOT INVOLVED IN THIS CASE AND THAT HE DOES NOT VIEW HER EMPLOYMENT AS A BASIS FOR RECUSAL. NO OBJECTIONS WERE MADE. ULING: AFTER HEARING THE EVIDENCE PRESENTED, THE COURT ORDERED THAT ASHWIN V. LADVA'S MOTION TO BE RELIEVED AS COUNSEL OF RECORD FOR PLAINTIFF LISA ROSE MALEPEAI IS GRANTED ON THE CONDITION THAT COUNSEL OBTAINS AN EXTENSION OF TIME FOR THE JUNE 3, 2008 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE HEARING OR HANDLE THAT ON THE PLAINTIFF'S BEHALF. Off Calendar Aug-26-2008 Set For Case Management Conference On Oct-17-08 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 212

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Issue Sanctions Or In The Alt/Terminating Sanctions For Failure To Comply With Court Order;
Off Calendar Aug-05-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: NO APPEARANCES ON THE MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: Defendant City And County Of San Francisco's Motion For Issue Sanctions, or in the alternative, Terminating Sanctions For Failure To Comply With Court Order is granted as it seeks terminating sanctions. No opposition has been filed. The court finds that additional orders to respond to discovery would not result in compliance. COMMISSIONER EVERETT A. HEWLETT, JR. MATTER NOT REPORTED

CONT. ARBITRATION ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE

RULING: AFTER HEARING THE EVIDENCE PRESENTED, THE COURT ORDERED THAT ASHWIN V. MINI MINUTES: MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD FOR PLAINTIFF LISA MALEPEAI IS OFF CALENDAR. LOCAL RULE 8.2(A)(2)(B) PROHIBITS SETTING A MOTION IN THIS DEPARTMENT ON THE SAME DAY A MOTION IS SET IN ANOTHER DEPARTMENT. Judge: Peter J. Busch, Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR# 8189 (301/PJB)

Notice Of Motiona Nd Motion To Compel Responses To Form Interrogatories, Special Interrogatories, Request For Production Of Documents, And Request For Statement Of Damages
Off Calendar Apr-07-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: NO APPEARANCES. TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED. Defendant's Motion To Compel Responses To Form Interrogatories, Special Interrogatories, Request For Production Of Documents, And Request For Statement Of Damages is granted. Plaintiff is ordered to respond to Defendant's Form Interrogatories, Special Interrogatories, Request For Production Of Documents, And Request For Statement Of Damages within 30 days of notice of this order. Plaintiff's failure to respond was a misuse of the discovery process. Sanctions are imposed against Plaintiff in the amount of $320, to be paid within 30 days of notice of this order. COMM: EVERETT A. HEWLETT. MATTER NOT REPORTED
